insight: Argentina's performance during hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup has been a mixed bag, with the team managing to gain momentum in only half of the breaks they've had, resulting in a slight decline in performance overall. When Argentina was pressing before a break, they tended to lose momentum, with their performance declining at an average rate of 0.400 minutes, and they were unable to regain their footing after the break. In contrast, when Argentina wasn't pressing before a break, they actually managed to slightly improve their performance, gaining momentum at an average rate of 0.100 minutes. This suggests that Argentina may benefit from taking a more conservative approach before hydration breaks in order to come out stronger afterwards.
